description: A person, community, or ecosystem is 'wolu' when the functions that sustain its life are in sound condition. Health takes its shape from the being and context at hand, not from one prescribed body. 'sunai' (health) is the condition itself, while 'towe' (well) commonly reports how someone is now.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then continue into stressed true-mid back rounded 'o'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l', let air pass along its sides, and raise the back of the tongue for high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: Both syllables keep rounded back vowels, joined by the open passage around lateral 'l'. The uninterrupted voiced flow resembles easy, unstrained breath, a mnemonic rather than a test of health.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A forest and a community can each be 'wolu' when their relations let life continue. Solarpunk work has reason to examine health at both scales.

semantic_domains.community: collective conditions that support people in continuing life
semantic_domains.nature: sound life-supporting relations in an organism or ecosystem
semantic_domains.physical: a living body's health in the context relevant to that body
